body_txt: It's a decades-old geek joke that the perfect computer would only have one instruction: Do What I Want. In The Future , this will be achieved, so that a character can push the same button over and over and have it do something different each time. (Often a Big Red Button , but not always) This is most commonly the result of a set constructed with lots of control panels, but no one keeping track of which button serves which purpose in the context of the story. These are often used to control the Do-Anything Robot . Compare Action Commands , Swiss-Army Weapon , Magic Tool , Damn You, Muscle Memory! . Sub-Trope of Plot-Sensitive Items . See also Context-Sensitive Button , in which the button is actively changing based on what's happening at the moment, and Handy Remote Control , a remote consisting of a single button that does different things each time it's pressed.
